articles.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
articles.description: The AI giant's co-founder explained why the company may tap the public market for capital and why tokenmaxxing pushback isn't a concern.

articles.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
articles.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

articles.title: Filtr is a new privacy tool that blocks ads in almost every iPhone and Mac app
articles.description: This popular ad blocker app for iPhones, iPads, and Macs can now block ads from loading inside apps, including web browsers, thanks to a new feature in the latest Apple software.

articles.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
articles.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure your spot today.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield is returning to Australia — here’s what happened the last time we came to Sydney
articles.description: On August 19, Startup Battlefield is returning to Sydney in partnership with Stripe, one of the world's most iconic technology companies. We're taking over Stripe Tour Sydney for a night that the Australian startup ecosystem won't forget.

articles.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
articles.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

articles.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
articles.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

articles.title: Helion, the Sam Altman-backed fusion startup, raises $465M to build a power plant for Microsoft
articles.description: Fusion startup Helion is racing to complete a power plant for Microsoft by 2028. A fresh infusion of cash should help with that.

articles.title: Meta’s Oversight Board says account bans lack due process, transparency
articles.description: Meta's board cites "due process" concerns over account bans. It's also pushing Meta to offer clear information about violations and its use in AI in making its determinations.

articles.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
articles.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"

articles.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
articles.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

articles.title: A burglar used a Waymo to steal yoga clothes in San Francisco — and got away with it
articles.description: The incident helps shed some new light on how Waymo treats and stores the footage captured by its robotaxis.

articles.title: Cash App launches a wand for tap-and-pay
articles.description: Digital wallet app Cash App is launching a new gadget on Thursday, seemingly inspired by the social media trend that involves paying for items in the real world with a tap of a homemade magic wand, which hides a tap-and-pay credit card.

articles.title: Waymo’s spent robotaxi batteries will be used as grid storage
articles.description: The company announced a deal with B2U Storage Solutions to repurpose the battery packs as Waymo pulls them off the road.

articles.title: Ramp raises $750M at $44B valuation as investors hunger for fintechs with an AI story
articles.description: Ramp has nearly tripled its valuation over the past year as investors scramble to grab a part of the fast-growing startup.

articles.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
articles.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

articles.title: Chinese spies are using LinkedIn to lure Westerners into sharing sensitive information
articles.description: The advisory warns that Chinese spies are using public job search platforms to recruit people with access to non-public information.

articles.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
articles.description: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

articles.title: Oura Ring 5 review: Thinner, lighter, better
articles.description: The Ring 5, which Oura describes as the world’s smallest smart ring, is 40% smaller than its predecessor and starts at $399.

articles.title: Benchmark raises its first-ever growth fund as part of $2B capital haul
articles.description: The legendary Silicon Valley VC firm abandons its more than 20-year tradition of keeping its funds to about $425 million.

articles.title: The skeptic’s guide to humanoid robots going viral on the Internet
articles.description: Robot demonstrations can distort public perceptions of robotic capabilities.

articles.title: AT&T and Verizon lose Supreme Court case over fines for selling location data
articles.description: FCC did not violate carriers' right to jury trial, court says in 8-1 ruling.

articles.title: These LLMs are the best at resisting Russian propaganda
articles.description: Estonian government benchmark shows how dozens of models combat Russia's "strategic narratives."

articles.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
articles.description: By targeting large numbers of users, attackers increased their chances of success.

articles.title: Elon Musk tries again to escape FTC audits of X data handling
articles.description: Musk can't be trusted to protect X user privacy, public commenters warn FTC.

articles.title: Cable lobby warns of chaos if FCC doesn't relax ban on foreign routers
articles.description: NCTA seeks waiver from foreign-router ban, citing memory and substrate shortages.

articles.title: Bumblebees can spontaneously solve problems, study finds
articles.description: Scientists in Finland found bees could solve an insect version of the classic "box-and-banana" problem.

articles.title: After 11 years at Mars, NASA's MAVEN spacecraft went out with a whisper
articles.description: “I think the team has really experienced the loss of a loved one with the end of the mission.”

articles.title: It doesn't feel very agricultural: The 2026 Subaru Solterra review
articles.description: Subaru's badge-engineered SUV remains on sale alongside the new Trailseeker.

articles.title: How some data center operators are tackling their water use problems
articles.description: Hyperscalers have come under scrutiny for their impact on water quality and availability.

articles.title: My SSN was exposed in a breach at Columbia—a school I have no connection with
articles.description: Columbia admits last year’s data breach exposed victims beyond its students, staff.

articles.title: Used Waymo robotaxi batteries become backup storage for power grids
articles.description: Used Waymo batteries will bolster California and Texas energy storage projects.

articles.title: Flesh-eating screwworm infection confirmed in South Texas, USDA says
articles.description: With the case confirmed, it is the fly's first breach of the US-Mexico border.

articles.title: Microsoft, Atom Computing update their quantum computing progress
articles.description: Some quantum computing companies we've covered have done recent progress updates.

articles.title: Google ordered to put clearer links in AI search and let UK publishers opt out
articles.description: Google must change AI Overviews after claiming users don't want "lots of sources."

articles.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
articles.description: Security advisory leaves out key details. Dashlane maintains complete silence.

articles.title: Google's new Gemma 4 12B model is designed to run on any laptop with 16GB of RAM
articles.description: Gemma 4 12B uses a new encoding scheme and token prediction to punch above its weight.

articles.title: Trump plan to test AI models has a problem—US security teams were gutted by DOGE
articles.description: Critics say Trump plan to test AI models is short-sighted, performative.

articles.title: New social features further Plex’s evolution from media server business
articles.description: Plex is increaingly focusing on content discovery and streaming rentals.

articles.title: Autonomous vehicles were supposed to cut traffic—what if they don't?
articles.description: Data shows Waymo's robotaxis are empty for almost half of the miles they drive.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与IPO动态**：Anthropic联合创始人Daniela Amodei在IPO前夕回应外界对AI投资回报的质疑；Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky宣布计划成立新AI实验室；Apple批准Poke作为Messages for Business平台首个AI代理。
2. **融资与估值**：Sam Altman支持的核聚变初创公司Helion获4.65亿美元融资，为微软建设发电站；企业支出管理平台Ramp完成7.5亿美元融资，估值达440亿美元，投资者青睐具有AI故事的金融科技公司。
3. **隐私与广告拦截**：新隐私工具Filtr上线，可在几乎所有iPhone和Mac应用中拦截广告。
4. **科技与基础设施**：Meta效仿特斯拉策略，在帐篷中建设数据中心；Waymo将退役的无人驾驶出租车电池用于电网储能。
5. **平台与监管**：Meta推出Facebook新AI创作者助手；其监督委员会指出账户封禁缺乏正当程序和透明度。

**最值得关注的发展**：AI领域的资本与产品创新持续加速，从IPO前的信心表态、新AI实验室的成立，到核聚变与金融科技的大额融资，显示出市场对AI技术及其商业回报的高度期待与投入。

articles.description: GPT-Rosalind advances life sciences research with enhanced biological reasoning, medicinal chemistry expertise, genomics analysis, and experimental workflow capabilities.

articles.description: OpenAI outlines a blueprint for U.S. governance of frontier AI, proposing a federal framework for safety, resilience, and national security.

articles.description: OpenAI outlines its public policy agenda for AI, including safety, youth protection, workforce transition, and global standards to ensure AI benefits society.

articles.title: Travelers deploys AI-powered claims countrywide with OpenAI
articles.description: Travelers built an AI-powered Claim Assistant with OpenAI to guide customers through filing claims, provide 24/7 support, and scale operations during peak demand.

articles.description: Discover new Codex plugins, sites, and annotations that help analysts, marketers, designers, investors, and other teams get more done with AI.

articles.description: OpenAI calls for global action on youth AI safety, proposing an international institute to strengthen safeguards, standards, and opportunities for young people.

articles.description: The Next Era of Knowledge Work report explores how Codex is transforming productivity through AI-powered research, data analysis, workflow automation, and content creation.

articles.description: Our approach to AI policy and political advocacy, transparency, support for thoughtful regulation and AI safety, and that no outside political group speaks on the company’s behalf.

articles.title: Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan
articles.description: OpenAI breaks ground on a 1GW data center project in Michigan as part of Stargate, building AI infrastructure to expand access, create jobs, and support communities.

articles.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
articles.description: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.

articles.title: Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
articles.description: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.

articles.title: How Braintrust turns customer requests into code with Codex
articles.description: How Braintrust engineers use Codex with GPT-5.5 to run experiments and code faster.

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **多模态安全与智能体优化**：英伟达发布Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety，支持企业级可定制多模态安全；Hugging Face推出EVA-Bench Data 2.0（覆盖3领域、121工具、213场景）及设计面向智能体的hf CLI工具。
2. **模型与工具新进展**：JetBrains发布12B参数的Mellum2混合专家模型；Holo3.1实现快速本地化计算机使用智能体；Reachy Mini新增MCP工具支持；Direct Preference Optimization研究拓展至聊天机器人之外场景。
3. **企业级AI落地关键**：强调超越大语言模型（LLM），可扩展的企业AI采纳依赖智能体逻辑；Endava展示如何围绕AI智能体重构软件交付流程。
4. **Google AI应用与原型**：Gemini助力构建Google I/O 2026；推出I/O 2026问答（由AI Studio生成）；展示9个Gemini Omni及3.5版实际演示；Futures Lab发布真实AI原型；Google搜索升级提升二手及复古购物体验。
5. **PyTorch性能分析入门**：发布PyTorch Profiler初学者指南（第一部分），助力开发者优化模型性能。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：企业AI正从单一模型能力转向以智能体逻辑、多模态安全与工具集成为核心的可扩展架构，Hugging Face、英伟达、Google及OpenAI均在此方向密集布局。
